title: Extract a Field With jq
description:
## Parse JSON on the Command Line

APIs and tools speak JSON, and **`jq`** is the standard tool for slicing it in scripts.

A file **`/root/data.json`** has been provided:

```json
{ "app": "web", "version": "2.3.1", "replicas": 3, "env": "prod" }
```

**Your task:** using `jq`, extract the value of the **`version`** field and write it (the raw value, **no quotes**) to **`/root/version.txt`**.

> 💡 `jq -r '.field'` prints a raw (unquoted) value. Redirect it into the file.

hints.title: Select a field
hints.body: jq '.version' selects the field; the -r flag prints it raw, without surrounding quotes.

validation.description: Checks that /root/version.txt holds the raw version value 2.3.1.
